blog.title: Product Notes and Operational Writing
blog.description: Use the blog for product rationale, architecture decisions, and release-operational changes that matter to users or admins.
blog.featuredDescription: ContextGo blog content should answer three questions clearly: what changed, why it matters, and how it affects real workflows.

entries.title: Why ContextGo starts from context before agents
entries.summary: The product is not trying to create another chat surface first. It starts by connecting the working context so agents can operate inside real workflows.

entries.title: Release operations should flow through contextgo-releases
entries.summary: The website, download center, and in-app updater all become simpler when one release repository acts as the canonical product-release source of truth.

entries.title: Desktop host, mobile client: keep the product model explicit
entries.summary: Remote access only feels coherent when users understand that the desktop machine remains the host and the mobile shell stays a remote client.
